examples.title: Recursive Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Implementation of inorder traversal using recursion - visits left subtree, then root, then right subtree
examples.code:
class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self, val):
        self.val = val
        self.left = None
        self.right = None

def inorder_recursive(root):
    if root:
        inorder_recursive(root.left)
        print(root.val, end=' ')
        inorder_recursive(root.right)

# Example usage:
#      50
#     /  \
#   30    70
#  /  \  /  \
#20  40 60  80
# Output: 20 30 40 50 60 70 80
examples.explanation: The recursive approach follows Left-Root-Right order. Time complexity is O(n) as each node is visited once. Space complexity is O(h) due to recursion stack, where h is tree height.

examples.title: Iterative Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Inorder traversal without recursion using an explicit stack data structure
examples.explanation: Uses a stack to simulate recursion. First pushes all left children, then pops to visit, then moves right. Avoids recursion stack overflow for deep trees.

examples.title: Level Order Traversal
examples.description: Breadth-First Search traversal of binary tree using queue
examples.explanation: Uses FIFO queue. Enqueue root, then repeatedly dequeue node, print it, enqueue its children. Processes level by level from left to right.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w is maximum width

examples.title: Morris Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Inorder traversal with O(1) space using temporary threaded links
examples.explanation: Morris traversal modifies the tree temporarily by creating threads to inorder predecessor. It finds way back without stack, then removes threads. Achieves O(1) auxiliary space.
